title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: The US and Iran reached a Pakistan-brokered two-week ceasefire agreement on April 7, 2026, with President Trump announcing a halt to US and Israeli strikes on Iran in exchange for Tehran reopening the Strait of Hormuz to shipping, amid over a month of prior escalatory airstrikes and retaliations. The truce, holding for its first day without major incidents, now faces strains from Iranian claims of US violations, disputes over its application to Israeli operations in Lebanon, and lingering tensions at the Strait. Negotiations are slated to start Friday in Islamabad, where progress on a permanent deal could solidify de-escalation, while any fresh military actions or diplomatic breakdowns risk unraveling the pause.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket crowns Rory McIlroy the slim favorite at 23% implied probability to win The Masters, edging Scottie Scheffler (18.5%) amid McIlroy's sharp ball-striking form in recent stroke-play events and strong Augusta course history, while Scheffler's odds have slipped from recent inconsistencies despite his two green jackets. Xander Schauffele (8%) lurks as a steady major performer with top-10 earnings edge, but Collin Morikawa's back injury clouds his top-20 streak. Favorable weather with light winds sets up firm Augusta National conditions favoring precision over power, withdrawals like Tiger Woods thin the 91-player field, and no dominant lead-up winner keeps this leaderboard battle wide open for upsets among PGA Tour heavyweights.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's unbeaten run through UEFA Group E qualifiers (5 wins, 1 draw, +19 goal difference), building on their Euro 2024 triumph and Lamine Yamal's emerging stardom, positions them as slim trader favorites at 15.4% implied probability for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with France (14.8%) and England (11.3%) close behind after their own dominant campaigns—France unbeaten in Group D (+12 GD) and England perfect in Group K (+22 GD). The tight clustering at the top reflects all major contenders securing spots by late March 2026 without upsets, alongside Argentina (9%) and Brazil (8.5%) navigating grueling CONMEBOL play to qualify in the top six despite Brazil's mid-pack finish. In the expanded 48-team field co-hosted by North American nations, uncertainties around the recent group draw, player fitness, and summer form keep the wisdom-of-crowds consensus evenly distributed among Europe's elite squads and South America's pedigree.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 26% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, driven by a March 12 Politico poll trouncing Kamala Harris in their home-state primary and his national book tour sparking frontrunner buzz amid post-2024 party recalibration. Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 9% on progressive appeal and social media reach, though centrists mobilized in South Carolina last month to block left-wing nominees like her. Sen. Jon Ossoff's 6% reflects viral anti-Trump rhetoric boosting his swing-state electability. This fragmented field could consolidate via 2026 midterm gains, fundraising edges, key endorsements, or personal announcements clarifying paths to victory in early primary states like New Hampshire and South Carolina.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election winner at 18.8% implied probability, just ahead of California Governor Gavin Newsom at 16.6%, with Senator Marco Rubio third at 9.3%, underscoring a fluid early-stage race dominated by speculation rather than primaries. Vance's lead stems from his vice presidential platform and strong Republican nominee odds around 37%, bolstered by a recent interview expressing confidence against Democratic challengers and a modest rebound after March volatility amid policy debates. Newsom's near-parity reflects his commanding leads in March 2026 Democratic primary polls, like 28% in California surveys, and public hints at a post-midterm bid during SXSW appearances. The tight field persists due to uncertainty ahead of November 2026 midterms, which could elevate governors or senators via battleground performances; separation may arise from midterm control of Congress, economic indicators, scandals, or early endorsements reshaping party nominations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Republican Presidential Nominee 2028 market prices Robert F. Kennedy Jr. narrowly ahead at 49%, reflecting his surging appeal as HHS Secretary through the MAHA movement and health policy focus, bolstered by recent family endorsements signaling a definite 2028 run and PAC maneuvers fueling speculation. Vice President J.D. Vance trails closely at 39.3% despite dominating March straw polls like CPAC (53%) and JL Partners surveys, with traders weighing his incumbency advantage against perceived foreign policy daylight amid Iran escalations. Secretary of State Marco Rubio holds third at 21.1%, gaining from high-profile diplomacy, as GOP donors quietly draft him. The tight race persists due to early-cycle volatility, Trump's lame-duck influence, and looming 2026 midterms that could reshape primaries, with Trump endorsements or scandal risks poised to create separation.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Péter Magyar at 69.5% to become Hungary's next prime minister following the April 12 parliamentary election, reflecting recent opinion polls where his centre-right Tisza party leads Viktor Orbán's incumbent Fidesz by widening margins, such as Medián's latest 58%-35% among decided voters—up from a 20-point gap in February. Magyar, a former government insider turned opposition challenger, has surged on promises of anti-corruption reforms and economic renewal amid voter fatigue after Orbán's 16 years in power. U.S. Vice President JD Vance's Budapest visit this week aimed to bolster Orbán but failed to reverse Tisza's momentum, with Hungary's first-past-the-post system still leaving room for Fidesz advantages in seat distribution despite national polling trends.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

markets.question: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile two-week ceasefire between the United States, Israel, and Iran, agreed on April 8, 2026, has held without direct fire exchanges as of April 9, amid efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for shipping. However, the truce faces immediate tests from Israel's massive airstrikes on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon—killing over 250 and prompting Iranian accusations of violations—while U.S. and Israeli officials insist the agreement excludes Lebanon. President Trump has ramped up pressure on Tehran for compliance, with upcoming U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks and Israel-Lebanon negotiations potentially determining de-escalation or renewed escalation. Gulf states report ongoing drone threats, underscoring persistent regional tensions driving trader assessments of conflict resolution timelines.

title: Counter-Strike: TheMongolz vs PARIVISION (BO3) - PGL Bucharest Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 4 match between TheMongolz and PARIVISION in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "TheMongolz" if TheMongolz win the match against PARIVISION.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION win the match against TheMongolz.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map Handicap: PRV (-1.5) vs TheMongolz (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 4 match between TheMongolz and PARIVISION in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "PARIVISION" if PARIVISION wins 2 or more maps than TheMongolz in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "TheMongolz". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: TheMongolz enter the PGL Bucharest 2026 playoffs quarterfinal BO3 against PARIVISION with superior group stage momentum, posting a 3-1 record including clean 2-0 sweeps over Wildcard, EYEBALLERS, and BC.Game after a lone loss to Astralis, underscoring their consistency as a top-8 ranked squad led by Techno and 910. PARIVISION, ranked around top-7, barely secured the final playoff spot via a grueling 2-0 decider over Wildcard—featuring a record-tying marathon OT on Mirage (28-26) and a comeback 13-11 on Dust2—exposing vulnerabilities despite strengths from Jame and zweih, following 3-2 group results with losses to FUT and 3DMAX. First major head-to-head favors no clear edge, but map vetoes loom large: PARIVISION excels on Ancient (82% win rate), while TheMongolz hold Mirage (59%). No roster changes or meta shifts noted in the past 48 hours.

title: Maple Leafs vs. Islanders
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 9 at 6:45PM ET:
If the Maple Leafs win, the market will resolve to "Maple Leafs".
If the Islanders win, the market will resolve to "Islanders".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Islanders host the battered Toronto Maple Leafs at UBS Arena in a pivotal late-season clash, with the Isles (42-31-5) battling for playoff positioning while riding a four-game losing streak. Leafs goaltender Anthony Stolarz's lower-body injury from yesterday's 4-0 shutout loss to Washington forces 24-year-old Artur Akhtyamov into his NHL debut against Ilya Sorokin, compounding absences of Auston Matthews, Brandon Carlo, Dakota Joshua, and Chris Tanev (season-ending core surgery). Toronto's 3-5-2 mark over their last 10 games highlights defensive woes, while new Isles coach Pete DeBoer's lineup debut features Bo Horvat centering Anders Lee and Simon Holmstrom, though Alexander Romanov remains sidelined with a shoulder issue. Home-ice edge and Toronto's depleted roster shape trader consensus.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders overwhelmingly price a 98% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate change at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, aligning closely with CME FedWatch Tool odds near 98%, reflecting the Fed's March 17-18 decision to hold the federal funds target range at 3.5%-3.75% amid resilient economic data. Strong March nonfarm payroll growth of 178,000—exceeding softer expectations—drove unemployment to 4.3%, while updated projections flagged sticky inflation at 2.7% year-end due to energy price spikes, tempering cut expectations. Powell's recent remarks emphasized balanced risks, supporting the pause. Realistic challenges include a hotter-than-expected March CPI release this week or escalating geopolitical tensions boosting oil, potentially tilting toward a hike; conversely, sharp labor weakening could revive cut odds before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder command 41.5% implied probability as 2026 NBA Champion favorites after clinching the league's best 64-16 record and #1 overall playoff seed with homecourt advantage throughout, sealed by their April 8 win amid a dominant late-season surge including 12 straight victories earlier. San Antonio Spurs trail at 14.8% with a locked #2 Western Conference seed at 61-19, propelled by Victor Wembanyama's March-leading PER of 32.3 and consistent elite form. Boston Celtics hold 11.7% as East #2 at 54-25, drawing on playoff pedigree despite Detroit Pistons' top East mark of 58-22. Denver Nuggets' 10.5% stems from a 10-game win streak boosting their #3 West position at 52-28.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 3 - April 10, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 3 12:00 PM ET to April 10, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 240-259 Elon Musk X posts from April 3-10 at 61% implied probability, driven by XTracker data showing 221 posts through April 9 (7 days elapsed) at a 32-post daily average—slightly off March's 42/day pace but steady amid Tesla FSD video virality, Grok benchmark wins, and defenses of X's open-source algorithm. This positions 260-279 (21.5%) as the next cluster, reflecting historical patterns where Musk's social media engagement surges with AI debates, SpaceX teases, or platform controversies. With under 20 hours left to April 10's close, any late-breaking xAI or Neuralink announcements could push toward 280+, though current momentum suggests restraint versus peak weeks. Markets capture skin-in-the-game bets on his unpredictable yet prolific posting rhythm.

title: Celtics vs. Knicks
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 9 at 7:30PM ET:
If the Celtics win, the market will resolve to "Celtics".
If the Knicks win, the market will resolve to "Knicks".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

markets.question: Spread: Knicks (-4.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 9 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Knicks" if the Knicks win the game by 5 or more points.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Celtics". If the game ends in a tie, this market will resolve to "Celtics".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Celtics enter their pivotal Eastern Conference matchup at Madison Square Garden against the New York Knicks hampered by key injuries, with Jaylen Brown ruled out (left Achilles tendinitis), Derrick White (right knee contusion) and Neemias Queta (right toe sprain) listed as questionable on the first night of a back-to-back. Jayson Tatum is available, but the absences test Boston's depth as they seek to clinch the No. 2 playoff seed with a win, holding a 54-25 record. The Knicks (51-28), largely healthy and riding a five-game home winning streak, present a formidable challenge in this late-season tilt with seeding ramifications for both squads.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil spot prices hover around $98 per barrel as of April 9, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on tight near-term supply amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions and surging U.S. exports to Europe under Russian sanctions, which propelled Q1 averages above $100 before a recent U.S.-Iran ceasefire eased tensions and triggered a pullback from intraday highs near $103. Elevated EIA short-term forecasts underscore robust Chinese demand signals offsetting OPEC+ output boosts and persistent global inventory builds projected at 3.1 million bpd for 2026. Key swing factors include weekly EIA inventory releases—due Thursday—and potential policy shifts from upcoming IEA and OPEC reports, with volatility heightened by geopolitical risks and macroeconomic risk appetite tied to Treasury yields.

title: Next Prime Minister of Denmark?
description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of Denmark following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the March 24, 2026, snap parliamentary election in Denmark's Folketing, where Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats secured the largest share of seats at 38 despite a historic vote slump to 21.9%, King Frederik X appointed her as formateur on March 25 to lead coalition negotiations as caretaker prime minister. Her red bloc holds 86 of 179 seats, short of the 90 needed for majority, positioning Moderates leader Lars Løkke Rasmussen as potential kingmaker open to centrist deals excluding extremes like the Danish People's Party. Trader consensus at 92.5% for Frederiksen reflects her first-mover advantage, prior dealmaking success, and endorsements from Faroese and Greenland MPs as of late March, with talks ongoing into April absent breakdowns. Realistic challenges include stalled red-bloc pacts prompting a blue-bloc alternative or fresh snap election.

title: 76ers vs. Rockets
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 9 at 8:00PM ET:
If the 76ers win, the market will resolve to "76ers".
If the Rockets win, the market will resolve to "Rockets".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Philadelphia 76ers center Joel Embiid's sudden appendicitis diagnosis and surgery Thursday in Houston rules him out of the matchup against the Houston Rockets at Toyota Center, severely impacting Philly's interior scoring and rebounding with the team at 43-36 amid Eastern Conference playoff positioning. The Rockets (50-29), battling for a top Western Conference seed alongside the Lakers, enter as home favorites despite missing point guard Fred VanVleet (ACL repair) and center Steven Adams (ankle surgery), with forward Tari Eason questionable. Philly's Cam Payne remains sidelined by a hamstring strain, tilting trader consensus toward Houston's depth, recent momentum, and rest advantages in this late-season tilt with playoff implications.

title: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Ninjas in Pyjamas (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 9 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

In the upcoming match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 9 at 5:00AM ET:

markets.question: First Blood in Game 2?

markets.question: LoL: Weibo Gaming vs Ninjas in Pyjamas (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 9 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Weibo Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 9 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win Game 1 against Weibo Gaming.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 9 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Weibo Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Penta Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Penta Kill occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

title: Colombia Presidential Election
description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M a slim favorite at 49.5% to win Colombia's May 31 presidential election first round or overall, edging Paloma Valencia (42.4%) and Iván Cepeda Castro (36.5%) in a fragmented field of over a dozen contenders. This tight race stems from March 8 congressional elections yielding a divided Congress, with Cepeda securing the left's Pacto Histórico primary amid President Petro's recent approval resurgence, while the right remains split between Valencia's center-right momentum and Abelardo de la Espriella's base. Polls show Cepeda leading first-round voting averages around 35-40%, but traders bet on moderate consolidation and runoff dynamics favoring Candidate M's appeal to swing voters wary of polarization. Key separators include pre-election endorsements, security incidents echoing past violence like Miguel Uribe's 2025 assassination, economic pressures, and campaign debates.

title: Crystal Palace FC vs. ACF Fiorentina

eventMetadata.context_description: Crystal Palace FC secured a commanding 3-0 victory over ACF Fiorentina in the first leg of their UEFA Europa Conference League quarter-final at Selhurst Park, propelling trader consensus to a near-certain 100% implied probability for a Palace win as markets await official resolution. Jean-Philippe Mateta opened the scoring from the penalty spot in the 24th minute after Evann Guessand was fouled, Tyrick Mitchell doubled the lead seven minutes later from close range, and Ismaïla Sarr sealed it with a 90th-minute header from Daichi Kamada's cross. Palace's high-energy flank attacks via Sarr and Daniel Muñoz, coupled with midfield control from Kamada and Adam Wharton, overwhelmed a Fiorentina side missing Moise Kean and lacking cutting edge despite second-half possession. Only an improbable administrative reversal could challenge this outcome.

title: Military action against Iran ends on...?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next date Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30), on which neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate. 

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. If strikes could be verified under the prior rule for each listed day, this market will resolve to "Military action through April 30."

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Military action against Iran ends before April 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the next date Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30), on which neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate. 

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. If strikes could be verified under the prior rule for each listed day, this market will resolve to "Military action through April 30."

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: Military action against Iran ends on

eventMetadata.context_description: US CENTCOM's announcement of pausing offensive operations against Iran after over 13,000 airstrikes marks a generational military defeat for Tehran, aligning with a fragile US-Iran ceasefire that took effect April 8 amid President Trump's peace terms. Traders' 98% consensus on April 9 reflects no verified new strikes or retaliations reported today—the first such pause in weeks—despite Strait of Hormuz tensions and Israeli actions against Hezbollah in Lebanon threatening de-escalation. This skin-in-the-game pricing anticipates market resolution absent resumption, though late-breaking escalations, ceasefire violations, or failed diplomatic talks could prompt renewed military action and shift odds dramatically.

eventMetadata.context_description: The first leg of the UEFA Europa League quarter-final between FC Porto and Nottingham Forest FC concluded in a 1-1 draw at Estádio do Dragão, propelling trader consensus to price the draw outcome at virtually 100% implied probability on Polymarket. Porto struck first in the 11th minute through William Gomes' close-range finish, dominating possession and shots thereafter, but Martim Fernandes' comical own goal gifted Forest an equalizer, securing a resilient away result despite their underdog status. This reflects the wisdom of crowds post-match, with prior Nottingham Forest's 2-0 league-phase win over Porto adding context to their unbeaten H2H run. Official UEFA confirmation could finalize resolution, though disputes over the scoreline or VAR reviews remain highly improbable scenarios to shift odds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes head to Chicago atop the Metropolitan Division after clinching the title with an overtime win over the Bruins, now chasing the Eastern Conference's No. 1 seed for full playoff home-ice advantage in their final regular-season road test. Blackhawks, eliminated at 28-36-14, open a four-game homestand featuring Connor Bedard centering Teuvo Teravainen and Landon Slaggert, with Frank Nazar riding momentum from his recent goal and assist versus San Jose. Carolina lists goaltender Pyotr Kochetkov injured, likely turning to Frederik Andersen or Brandon Bussi behind Andrei Svechnikov-Sebastian Aho-Seth Jarvis; Chicago shelves Oliver Moore (lower body), Matt Grzelcyk, and Artyom Levshunov. Hurricanes' elite depth, top power play, and seeding stakes outweigh Chicago's home ice and youth upside in trader consensus.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent U.S. airstrikes on April 7 targeted military installations on Kharg Island, Iran's critical oil export terminal handling over 90% of its crude shipments in the Persian Gulf, but deliberately spared oil and gas infrastructure. Iranian state media, including Mehr News, confirmed the island remains fully under armed forces control with exports uninterrupted, countering claims of major damage. This follows a March U.S. bombing raid and reflects escalating U.S.-Iran tensions amid President Trump's deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Traders weigh risks of further military action, potential ground operations, or diplomatic de-escalation that could shift control dynamics before the market's resolution date.

title: Heat vs. Raptors
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 9 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Heat win, the market will resolve to "Heat".
If the Raptors win, the market will resolve to "Raptors".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

markets.question: Bam Adebayo: Points O/U 21.5

eventMetadata.context_description: Toronto Raptors (44-35, sixth in Eastern Conference) host Miami Heat (41-38, 10th) in the second of back-to-back games at Scotiabank Arena, after dominating 121-95 on April 7—a result locking Heat into the play-in tournament for the fourth straight year while boosting Toronto's direct playoff push. Raptors enter healthy with no injury concerns, led by Scottie Barnes and Brandon Ingram's recent form, holding a season sweep edge over Miami. Heat grapple with key absences—Nikola Jović (ankle, out), Terry Rozier (not with team, out)—and questionables Norman Powell (groin) and Dru Smith (foot), plus road back-to-back fatigue, heightening Toronto's home-court and rest advantages in this late-season seeding battle.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Finland at 39% implied probability to win Eurovision 2026 in Vienna, propelled by the February 28 UMK victory of Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's high-energy rock anthem "Liekinheitin," which fuses classical violin flair with pop-rock theatrics to appeal across juries and televotes—bolstered by leading OGAE fan votes, streaming surges, and broad previews. France's 11.3% trails with 17-year-old soprano Monroe's operatic "Regarde!," internally selected March 6 amid performance buzz, while Denmark's 10.2% reflects Søren Torpegaard Lund's emotive Danish-language "Før vi går hjem" from DMGP, despite late-March pro-Israel sticker backlash. With semis on May 12/14 and final May 16, running order reveals and rehearsals could spark shifts in this competitive field.

title: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires that constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires that only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A peace deal or political framework will qualify if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, effective on a specific date. Frameworks or agreements that outline terms for a future peace but do not include an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not count.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by April 30 at just 4%, reflecting persistent frontline escalation and stalled diplomatic efforts amid ongoing military operations. Russian forces continue drone strikes targeting Ukrainian civilians, as reported in recent assessments, while both sides claim territorial advances without broader de-escalation signals. US-brokered trilateral talks, proposed in March, remain on hold due to Middle East distractions like the Iran conflict. President Putin's April 9 announcement of a limited Orthodox Easter truce—from 4 p.m. April 11 to sunset April 12—offers only a brief pause, dismissed by Moscow as insufficient for lasting peace without Ukrainian concessions on occupied territories. Realistic shifts would require sudden high-level negotiations or a major battlefield reversal, though incompatible demands sustain high trader confidence in no resolution by month's end.

title: Panthers vs. Senators
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 9 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Panthers win, the market will resolve to "Panthers".
If the Senators win, the market will resolve to "Senators".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Florida Panthers head to Ottawa severely depleted by injuries, with captain Aleksander Barkov, Sam Reinhart, Anton Lundell, Aaron Ekblad, Dmitry Kulikov, Niko Mikkola, and others sidelined, forcing coach Paul Maurice to scramble lines and defense pairings amid a season-ending spoiler role after playoff elimination. The Senators, clinging to a wild card spot in the Atlantic Division with a 38-26-10 record, welcome back Thomas Chabot from a broken arm just 17 days post-surgery, bolstering their blue line for home-ice advantage at Canadian Tire Centre. Despite Florida's 6-3 win over Ottawa on March 31, the Panthers' 37-37-4 mark, recent shootout loss to Montreal, and road fatigue contrast Ottawa's 6-3-1 run in their last 10, shaping trader consensus toward the motivated hosts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Both Chicago Bulls and Washington Wizards enter their April 9 matchup at Capital One Arena eliminated from playoffs, with the Wizards at 17-62 and Bulls at 30-49 amid late-season losing skids. Chicago dominated the April 7 rematch 129-98, fueling trader consensus favoring the visitors despite depleted rosters on both sides. Bulls rule out Josh Giddey (hamstring management) and Matas Buzelis (illness), with Isaac Okoro sidelined by quad contusion; Patrick Williams is probable (thumb). Wizards miss Bilal Coulibaly (heel), Jaden Hardy (back), and Tristan Vukcevic (knee IR), plus Alex Sarr (questionable, toe), leaning on rookies like Julian Reese amid a back-to-back home stand. Recent injury reports underscore high uncertainty in this low-stakes clash.
